steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the definition of an equilateral triangle
steps.content: An equilateral triangle has all three sides of equal length. To prove triangle ABC is equilateral, we must establish that AB = BC = AC.

steps.title: Determine what AC must equal to satisfy the equilateral condition
steps.content: We are given AB = 4,720 mm and BC = 4,720 mm. Since we need all three sides to be equal, AC must equal AB and BC. Therefore, AC must equal 4,720 mm. That is the correct answer.
